questions.question: What was the original purpose of the CSS float property?
questions.explanation: Float was originally designed to wrap text around images, like in newspaper layouts.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid float value?
questions.explanation: Float only accepts: left, right, none, and inherit. There is no 'center' value for float.

questions.question: What happens to a parent container when all its children are floated?
questions.explanation: When all children are floated, the parent container collapses because floated elements are removed from the normal flow.

questions.question: Which modern CSS features should replace float for page layout?
questions.explanation: Flexbox and CSS Grid are the modern, recommended methods for page layout. Float should be reserved for text wrapping.

questions.question: What is required for a floated element to have predictable behavior?
questions.explanation: Floated elements almost always need an explicit width. Without one, they may take unexpected widths based on content.

questions.question: How does text behave around a float: left element?
options: Text wraps to the right and below
Text moves to the right only
questions.explanation: With float: left, the element moves to the left edge, and text wraps around its right side and below.

questions.question: What is a clearfix used for?
questions.explanation: Clearfix is a technique to make a parent container properly contain its floated children, preventing the height collapse.

questions.question: Which CSS property is related to float and controls the behavior after it?
questions.explanation: The clear property specifies whether an element can be next to floated elements or must move below them.

questions.question: What is a drop cap in typography?
options: An enlarged first letter that drops into the text
questions.explanation: A drop cap is a decorative effect where the first letter is enlarged and floats into the text. CSS achieves this with ::first-letter and float.

questions.question: How do floated elements interact with block-level elements?
options: Block elements ignore floats and overlap them
questions.explanation: Block-level elements ignore floated elements and render behind them. Only inline content wraps around floats.

questions.question: What is the default value of the float property?
questions.explanation: The default value of float is 'none', meaning the element does not float and remains in the normal flow.

questions.question: Can you float a flex item?
options: No, float is ignored on flex items
Only if display: block is set
questions.explanation: Float does not work on flex items. The float property is ignored when the element is a flex item.

questions.question: Which pseudo-element is commonly used for the clearfix technique?
questions.explanation: The clearfix technique uses ::after with content: '' and clear: both to clear floats within a parent container.

questions.question: What happens to a float: right element in a left-to-right document?
options: It moves to the left edge
It moves to the right edge, text wraps on left
questions.explanation: float: right moves the element to the right edge of its container, and text wraps around its left side.

questions.question: What is a pull quote?
options: A quote pulled from text and displayed prominently
questions.explanation: A pull quote is a key excerpt from an article displayed more prominently, often floated to one side for visual appeal.

questions.question: How does float affect an element's display property?
questions.explanation: When an element is floated, its computed display value becomes 'block' for elements that would normally be inline.

questions.question: What is the main accessibility concern with floated layouts?
options: Visual order may differ from DOM order
questions.explanation: Floats can create a visual order that differs from the DOM order, which can be confusing for screen reader users.

questions.question: What is the best modern alternative to float for multi-column layouts?
questions.explanation: CSS Grid is the most powerful modern method for creating multi-column layouts, far more capable than float-based layouts.

questions.question: Which of these is a proper clearfix implementation?
questions.explanation: The proper clearfix uses ::after pseudo-element with content, display: table (or block), and clear: both.

questions.question: When should you use float instead of Flexbox?
questions.explanation: Float should be used primarily for text wrapping around elements (images, pull quotes, drop caps). Flexbox and Grid are better for everything else.
